description: 'wethalu' covers any item made to be worn on the body. As the general clothing word, it is broad enough for a cloak or a pair of trousers and for garments tied to a particular practice. 'wetha' (cloth) is a material or piece of material; a garment is defined by being worn.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips for voiced 'w' and move into mid-front 'e'. Place the tongue tip at the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', release into stressed open 'a', then touch the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and finish on high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The opening 'wetha' is the complete Phi word for cloth. The added final 'lu' lets the clothing word echo that familiar material.
usage_notes: A body part may precede the noun to describe where the garment is worn, as in 'koma wethalu' (head-garment). A culturally specific garment retains its source identity when shape, status, or community name matters.

semantic_domains.creation: an item shaped or assembled for wear
semantic_domains.physical: clothing worn on or around the body
